commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Physicians, whose business it was to embalm dead bodies, with a composition of myrrh, in order to keep them from putrefaction, (Menochius) as the Egyptian mummies are treated. (Haydock) The entrails are taken out, by the embalmer during 30 days, and the body is left in salt and various drugs, for other 40, in all 70 days, as Herodotus informs us, (B. 86,) and as Moses here insinuates, ver. This was an honour peculiar to the kings. Before any person was buried, his praises were rehearsed; and it was lawful on this occasion to declare, what evil even the kings themselves had done; which sometimes caused them to be deprived of funeral honours. We have several funeral canticles preserved in Scripture: 2 Kings i. 33; 2 Paralipomenon xxxv. (Calmet) The Lamentations of Jeremias were perhaps of this nature, on the death of King Josias. The usual time for mourning among the Jews, was 30 days for people of eminence, (Numbers xx. ; Deuteronomy xxxiv. 8; Procopius) and seven for the rest, Ecclesiasticus xxii.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
